name: Mathuranatha Tarkavagisa
dates_notes: Mathurānātha is dated to the late sixteenth and early seventeenth century by his place in the post-Raghunātha Navadvīpa Navya-Nyāya sub-commentarial line.
core_thesis: Mathurānātha Tarkavāgīśa is a Navadvīpa Navya-Nyāya commentator whose Māthurī glosses belong to the technical sub-commentarial stack on Gaṅgeśa and Raghunātha. His role is not to found a new school but to stabilize and extend the analytic language of Navya-Nyāya after the Dīdhiti. The importance of the Māthurī lies in its handling of definitions, delimiters, relations, and inferential conditions in the post-Raghunātha idiom. For Vedānta, Mīmāṃsā, and Vyākaraṇa materials, Mathurānātha helps represent the technical Nyāya language that early-modern scholastic opponents had to use or answer.

engaged_works.title: Tattvacintamani-Mathuri
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely associated with Mathurānātha in the Navya-Nyāya commentarial tradition.
engaged_works.summary: A technical Navya-Nyāya sub-commentarial work on the Tattva-Cintāmaṇi tradition. The Māthurī explains and refines the dense post-Gaṅgeśa analyses of perception, inference, comparison, and testimony, especially where the Dīdhiti leaves compact formulations requiring scholastic unpacking. Its importance is tied to the Navadvīpa curriculum: it is part of the apparatus by which later students learn the exact force of avacchedaka, pratiyogin, vyāpti, and related technical notions.

engaged_works.title: Didhiti-Mathuri
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Traditionally transmitted as Mathurānātha's gloss on Raghunātha Śiromaṇi's Dīdhiti.
engaged_works.summary: A sub-commentary on Raghunātha Śiromaṇi's Dīdhiti. The work belongs to the technical Navya-Nyāya practice of explaining a compact master-text through layered clarification, objection, and reformulation. It is important for documenting how Raghunātha's revisions to Gaṅgeśa were received and made teachable in the Navadvīpa scholastic line.
